title: put() Some branch sets cached_nvm_id redundantly after find_cached_or_scan
description: In put() Some branch: 'guard.cached_nvm_id = Some(id)' is a no-op — find_cached_or_scan already set it in all three of its return paths (cache-hit, stale+scan, no-cache+scan). The None branch correctly needs the explicit set because find_cached_or_scan returns None and the free_id is new. The asymmetry is confusing: readers may wonder whether the Some branch set is meaningful. Fix: add a comment on the Some branch explaining it is intentionally defensive ('find_cached_or_scan already set this; explicit here to make cache invariant visible at the call site'), or extract the cache update to after the match to make both branches symmetric.
